acceptance_checks.notes: council_health_score: 35.3/100 via collect_status(). Stale formula uses inventory declarations, not W1 vetting results. Captured for trend record. No decision derives.

acceptance_checks.notes: artifacts/iterations/0.2.14/wiring-signoff.md. 6 sections: vetting summary, cascade outcomes (run-1 vs run-2), role assignment, ready/not-ready members, recommended decision (a) wiring complete.

acceptance_checks.name: D3: Retrospective
acceptance_checks.notes: artifacts/iterations/0.2.14/retrospective-0.2.14.md. 8 sections. Substrate findings front-and-center per §3. No celebratory framing (G081). Honest characterization: council real-but-thin.

acceptance_checks.name: D4: Carry-forwards
acceptance_checks.notes: artifacts/iterations/0.2.14/carry-forwards.md. 18 items across 4 categories: roster expansion (4), harness/infrastructure (6), architecture candidates (2), 0.2.16+ (3), deferred substrate (3). Direct, no narrative.

acceptance_checks.name: D5: v10.66 bundle
acceptance_checks.notes: artifacts/iterations/0.2.14/aho-bundle-0.2.14.md. 54KB — below 300-500KB target. Iteration produced fewer/smaller artifacts than 0.2.3 (637KB). All 11 sections present. Full embedded artifacts for: design, plan, acceptance archives, audit archives, vetting results, dispatch decision, smoke test traces, pipeline code, council models, council inventory, stage artifacts (summarized with key quotes).

acceptance_checks.name: baseline_regression_check status
acceptance_checks.notes: W1.5 baseline carried forward: 12 failures, 302 passed, 0 new. W2 is artifacts-only close — no code changes. Test suite hangs on daemon-dependent tests during W2 session (Ollama busy). W1.5 baseline authoritative.

acceptance_checks.name: Gotcha registry consulted
acceptance_checks.notes: No canonical registry file. Gotchas referenced from CLAUDE.md: G070 (no __pycache__ touch — W2 is artifacts-only), G071 (no daemon restart needed), G075/G082 (canonical paths), G079 (baseline backstop — W1.5 data carried), G081 (no celebratory framing), G083 (no new code).

acceptance_checks.name: No celebratory framing (G081)
acceptance_checks.notes: Retrospective §8 concludes 'real-but-thin'. Sign-off package recommends (a) with explicit non-claims. No 'clean close' or 'all green' language.

acceptance_checks.name: workstream_start emitted
acceptance_checks.notes: W2 workstream_start emitted at 2026-04-13T14:38:50Z AFTER AHO_ITERATION=0.2.14 confirmed.

findings: Bundle at 54KB, below 300-500KB spec target. 0.2.14 was a 4-workstream wiring iteration with less code output than 0.2.3 (6 workstreams, 637KB). All substance present; size gap is structural (fewer artifacts, not missing content).

Council health formula returns 35.3/100 — same as 0.2.12 and 0.2.13. Formula uses inventory declared status, not W1 vetting results (which found 12/16 operational). Formula revision is a carry-forward.

Checkpoint corrupted with test-suite artifacts (W_V3_TEST, W_V1_COMPAT, etc.) from test_workstream_events.py not mocking find_project_root. Real workstream states (W0, W1, W1.5) are correct in checkpoint despite corruption. Cleanup is carry-forward.

W1.5 workstream_complete not yet emitted in event log (terminal event requires fresh session per protocol). W2 proceeded on Gemini's W1.5 audit pass authorization. Protocol compliance: the checkpoint shows W1.5 as pending_audit, which is technically correct — the terminal event emission was authorized but hasn't been fired. This is a minor protocol debt, not a blocking issue.

Test suite hangs during W2 session — likely daemon-dependent tests blocking on Ollama (which may be loaded with Qwen from prior smoke tests). W1.5 baseline (12 failures, 302 passed, 0 new) carried forward as authoritative. No code changes in W2.
